title: DNA Size Selection and Purification Using Improved Homemade SPRI Beads Solution
author: Ramawatar
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.n7hdhj6
materials: Make sure the beads are resuspend properly by flicking the tube gently and spinning it down - the solution will be homogeneous and brown (I put it in a 50°C Thermoblock for about 20 seconds to encourage the elution reaction)
1.0:0.8 V our SPRI beads with Tween-20 Lane
1.0:0.8 V our SPRI beads w/o Tween-20 Lane
1.0:1.0 V our SPRI beads with Tween-20 Lane
1.0:1.0 V our SPRI beads w/o Tween-20 Lane
1.0:1.5 V our SPRI beads with Tween-20 Lane
1.0:1.5 V our SPRI beads w/o Tween-20 Lane
1.0:2.0 V our PRI beads with Tween-20 Lane
1.0:2.0 V our SPRI beads w/o Tween-20

steps.title: For 10 mL beads stock solution
steps.instruction: For 10 mL beads stock solution: %s %s Final stock Input 10 mM Tris-HCl 1 M 100 μl 1 mM EDTA pH 8 0.5 M 20 μl 1.6 M NaCl 5 M 3.2 ml 11% PEG 8000 50% (w/v) 2.2 ml 0.25 % Tween-20 10 % (v/v) 200 0.4% beads (v/v) 100% 40 μl Milliq Water 4.24 ml Total 10 ml Frist combine only Water, Tris-HCl, EDTA and NaCl in a 50 mL tube.

steps.title: Vortex Sera-Mag SpeedBeads® Carboxyl Magnetic Beads...
steps.instruction: Vortex Sera-Mag SpeedBeads® Carboxyl Magnetic Beads (GE Healthcare) very well and pipette 40 μl into a 1.5 ml tube, put it on the magnetic rack and wait until the solution has cleared up and all beads have bound to the back of the tube

steps.title: Wash beads by removing supernatant and...
steps.instruction: Wash beads by removing supernatant and adding 1 ml Milli-Q water

steps.title: Take tube of the magnet, mix...
steps.instruction: Take tube of the magnet, mix well, spin down in a microcentrifuge and put back on the magnet

steps.title: Wait for beads to assemble at...
steps.instruction: Wait for beads to assemble at the back of the tube

steps.title: Repeat washing (steps 3 - 6) 3 more times
steps.instruction: Repeat washing (steps 3 - 6) 3 more times

steps.title: After pipetting of the supernatant the...
steps.instruction: After pipetting of the supernatant the last time take off tube from the magnet and add 40 μl of the previous (step 1) prepared stock solution, mix well, spin down and pipette everything into the remaining stock solution in the 50 mL tube and mix

steps.title: Now the 2
steps.instruction: Now the 2.2 ml 50% PEG can be added to the stock solution, which after vortexing very well is ready for use. Be careful to actually pipette 2.2 ml as solution is very viscous, but the final concentration of PEG is crucial for the clean up to work properly.

steps.title: Bring you DNA sample in a 1
steps.instruction: Bring you DNA sample in a 1.5 ml tube to comfortable pipetable volume (I usually do it with some volume between 20 - 200 μl) and if you know already that your sample contains a lot of contaminants and/or DNA (hence a really viscous solution) diluting it out and splitting into two tubes can make life easier. (A lot of contaminants can also clump around the beads which makes binding to the magnet slower and sometimes pipetting off the supernatant really difficult)

steps.title: Make sure to know the exact...
steps.instruction: Make sure to know the exact volume of your sample and add 1 V of that (or 0.9 V for removal of more smaller fragments but also higher risk of less recovery) of well homogenized, room temperature beads solution and mix by flicking the tube

steps.title: Place the tube on a rotor...
steps.instruction: Place the tube on a rotor (or mixer if you don't have one) and mix for 10 minutes

steps.title: Spin down tube in the microcentrifuge...
steps.instruction: Spin down tube in the microcentrifuge and place on the magnet

steps.title: Wait until beads have moved to...
steps.instruction: Wait until beads have moved to the back of the tube and the solution becomes clear (depending on viscosity of the solution that can take between 1 min to much longer like 1h, if after that the beads look like they are stuck in a big cloud and just don't properly bind to the magnet I add same amount of buffer and beads (the ratio always has to stay the same!) mix again and the put it back on the magnet - usually that solved the problem. But you probably will never have that problem, I just worked with really contaminated samples (plants))

steps.title: Remove and discard supernatant
steps.instruction: Remove and discard supernatant

steps.title: Wash beads with fresh 70% Ethanol...
steps.instruction: Wash beads with fresh 70% Ethanol by adding 1 - 1.5 mL to the opposite side than where the beads bind and wait 30 seconds

steps.title: Remove and discard Ethanol
steps.instruction: Remove and discard Ethanol

steps.title: Repeat washing once more (steps 15...
steps.instruction: Repeat washing once more (steps 15 - 18) (Don't remove tube from magnet during the washing steps until here)

steps.title: For the last removal of Ethanol...
steps.instruction: For the last removal of Ethanol make sure that all the Ethanol is removed, therefore take tube off the magnet , spin down for a second and place back onto the magnet, like that also the last drops of Ethanol can be pipetted off

steps.title: Let beads air dry for a...
steps.instruction: Let beads air dry for a maximum of 30 seconds or else elution will be difficult

steps.title: Add 50 ul (or in whatever...
steps.instruction: Add 50 ul (or in whatever final volume and concentration the sample is needed) of preheated to 50°C 10 mM Tris (or TE-Buffer)

steps.title: Spin down the tube before placing...
steps.instruction: Spin down the tube before placing it on the magnet again and wait until the beads have bound the the back of the tube (that can again take its time especially if the sample contains really long DNA fragments) Depending on the sample that will take between 1 minute to a few hours, I usually wait 5 - 10 minutes

steps.title: When the solution has cleared up...
steps.instruction: When the solution has cleared up completely, pipette the supernatant to a fresh tube and discard beads.

steps.title: Meassure the DNA concentration with a Qubit
steps.instruction: Meassure the DNA concentration with a Qubit. The recoveries for HMW DNA > 20kb should lie between 60 - 90%

steps.title: Bioline 1kb hyperladder Lane 2:0
steps.instruction: Bioline 1kb hyperladder Lane 2:0.45 AMPure XP Beads (Beckman Coulter). This is the standard dilution used in PacBio and Oxford Nanopore protocols. Lane

steps.title: Pipette off and discard supernatant
steps.instruction: Pipette off and discard supernatant
